title: Add full and allow-errors flags to http head
body:
## Summary

- Add `--full` (`-f`) and `--allow-errors` (`-e`) support to `http head` for parity with other HTTP verbs.
- When `--full` is used, `http head` now returns the standard full response record (urls, headers.request, headers.response, body, status).
- When `--allow-errors` is used, `http head` no longer fails on non-2xx statuses and still exposes headers.

## Implementation details

- Extended `crates/nu-command/src/network/http/head.rs`:
  - Added `full` and `allow_errors` to the command signature and `Arguments`.
  - Reused the shared HTTP response pipeline via `RequestFlags` and `request_handle_response` when `--full` is set.
  - Updated status handling to honor `allow-errors` for non-full responses.
- Added tests in `crates/nu-command/tests/commands/network/http/head.rs`:
  - `http_head_with_accept_errors` to ensure `-e` prevents failures on 4xx responses and still returns headers.
  - `http_head_full_response_includes_status_and_headers` to verify the full response shape and header presence.

## Notes

- Default `http head` behavior (no flags) is preserved: it still returns a table of headers and errors on non-2xx responses.
